sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Jack, presents with a complaint of neck pain, which started approximately a week ago after a car accident in which he was hit in the back and his neck was forced forward.
The pain is described as a 7 out of 10 in severity, and is exacerbated by movement of the neck.
He reports some improvement with ibuprofen, but still experiences pain when turning his head or flexing his neck.
He denies headaches, visual disturbances, and hearing problems.
He has a history of playing football years ago, but no prior neck issues.
Past medical history: The patient has no prior medical history mentioned.
Review of systems: The patient denies any symptoms in other organs that are relevant to his initial presentation.
Current medications: I prescribed Motrin 600mg every 6-8 hours for anti-inflammatory purposes, and Flexeril 10mg every 12 hours as needed for muscle relaxation.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient exhibits positive pain to palpation of the soft tissues of the neck, positive pain with flexion, severe positive pain with extension, and positive pain with rotation.
He is able to move his neck, but experiences pain with lateral bending.
Test Results: The x-ray results show no signs of fracture or bony abnormalities.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has neck strain, likely caused by whiplash from the car accident.
Problem list: Neck strain; Suspected whiplash.
Plan:
I plan to treat this conservatively with anti-inflammatory medication and muscle relaxation.
I will also order an MRI to rule out any underlying conditions such as concussion or nerve impingement.
I will refer the patient to physical rehab or a chiropractor for further evaluation and treatment once the MRI results are available.
Follow-up: I will review the MRI results and provide further treatment or referrals as necessary.
